key_insight: A physics-constrained graph-based LPTM can be identified directly from sparse temperature measurements and inputs, yielding a stable and physically meaningful thermal surrogate for spacecraft digital twins.
engineering_relevance: The approach offers a practical reduced-order thermal model that balances accuracy and computational efficiency for spacecraft thermal analysis and long-horizon prediction.
method: Formulate thermal calibration as a trajectory-matching inverse problem on a graph dynamical system.
Enforce physical admissibility by construction: positive nodal coefficients and symmetric conductive couplings.
Fit model parameters using measured temperature trajectories and known inputs without relying on material- or geometry-derived priors.
evidence_signal: The method was validated on synthetic datasets from high-fidelity finite element simulations, and it remained accurate over long time horizons and robust to measurement noise.
watch_item: Performance is demonstrated on synthetic FEM-generated data, so transfer to real spacecraft telemetry may require careful handling of model mismatch and sensor sparsity.
paper_title: Physics-constrained identification of graph-based thermal networks for spacecraft digital twins
